headline: Breastfeeding Rate at 6 Months Drops to 40% Below National Target
context: Exclusive breastfeeding rate at 6 months fell below the 50% national target set in the National Nutrition Policy.

cards.big: Only 40% of infants are exclusively breastfed at 6 months in 2025 below the 50% target
cards.sub: Rate declined from 47% in 2020 as workplace breastfeeding support eroded

cards.big: Only 28% of workplaces provide dedicated breastfeeding rooms despite MOH guidelines since 2016
cards.sub: Voluntary compliance produces inadequate workplace lactation support

cards.big: Employment Act allows 98-day maternity leave but WHO recommends 6 months for breastfeeding
cards.sub: Return to work at 3 months is the primary reason mothers stop breastfeeding early

cards.big: Norway's 49-week parental leave achieves 82% exclusive breastfeeding rate at 6 months
cards.sub: Extended paid leave is the single strongest predictor of breastfeeding duration

cards.big: Expecting mothers to breastfeed for 6 months while mandating only 3 months leave is contradictory policy
Should maternity leave be extended to 6 months to align with WHO breastfeeding recommendations
